De plus, Aquafil aborde activement la sécurité environnementale liée aux produits en développant la première norme ISO pour la mesure des microplastiques dans les textiles et en menant des efforts anti-greenwashing. Cependant, la divulgation manque de données quantitatives spécifiques sur les incidents de sécurité des produits, les rappels ou le nombre/taux de réclamations des clients.
score_explanation_english: The company achieves a score of 2 as it explicitly mentions product safety and quality control through its Integrated Management System (IMS) and ISO 9001 certification, covering 12 out of 13 production facilities and 93% of employees. It identifies 'Damage to final consumers' as a material risk related to product safety and manages customer complaints. Furthermore, Aquafil actively addresses product-related environmental safety by developing the first ISO standard for measuring microplastics in textiles and undertaking anti-greenwashing efforts. However, the disclosure lacks specific quantitative data on product safety incidents, recalls, or the number/rate of customer complaints.
score_explanation_italian: L'azienda ottiene un punteggio di 2 poiché menziona esplicitamente la sicurezza del prodotto e il controllo qualità tramite il suo Sistema di Gestione Integrato (IMS) e la certificazione ISO 9001, che copre 12 dei 13 stabilimenti di produzione e il 93% dei dipendenti. Identifica il 'Danno ai consumatori finali' come un rischio materiale relativo alla sicurezza del prodotto e gestisce i reclami dei clienti. Inoltre, Aquafil affronta attivamente la sicurezza ambientale legata ai prodotti sviluppando il primo standard ISO per la misurazione delle microplastiche nei tessuti e intraprendendo iniziative anti-greenwashing. Tuttavia, la divulgazione manca di dati quantitativi specifici sugli incidenti di sicurezza del prodotto, sui richiami o sul numero/tasso di reclami dei clienti.

user_friendly_explanation_italian: L'azienda ascolta attivamente i suoi dipendenti e utilizza il loro feedback per apportare miglioramenti. Conducono sondaggi annuali per comprendere il clima lavorativo in diverse regioni, e citano in particolare risultati positivi dai sondaggi in Slovenia e Croazia nel 2024. Utilizzano anche un questionario speciale ogni due anni per verificare lo stress lavoro-correlato e si incontrano regolarmente con i rappresentanti sindacali. Inoltre, dimostrano che questi sforzi di ascolto portano a cambiamenti reali, come l'utilizzo del feedback dalla loro 'analisi del clima aziendale' per implementare modalità di lavoro flessibile per ridurre lo stress. Utilizzano anche gli spunti dall'engagement dei dipendenti sulla 'Diversità' per guidare la formazione e le iniziative come le certificazioni di parità di genere.
user_friendly_explanation: The company actively listens to its employees and uses their feedback to make improvements. They conduct annual surveys to understand the workplace atmosphere in different regions, and specifically mention positive results from surveys in Slovenia and Croatia in 2024. They also use a special questionnaire every two years to check on work-related stress and regularly meet with union representatives. What's more, they show that these listening efforts lead to real changes, such as using feedback from their 'business climate analysis' to implement flexible work arrangements to reduce stress. They also use insights from employee engagement on 'Diversity' to guide training and initiatives like gender equality certifications.
information: Among the highlights of 2024, we conducted a Corporate Survey in the plants in Slovenia and Croatia, with positive and encouraging results. In addition to daily confrontation in the workplace, we have established several formal listening mechanisms including periodic meetings with union representatives, the business climate analysis and the Do Ut Des. These tools also allow us to measure the effectiveness of our engagement process and to monitor any groups of 'at-risk' workers that have not been identified so far.

The first tool is the Corporate Survey, a climate analysis that we conduct every year in different geographical areas. In 2024, the focus was on plants in Slovenia and Croatia, with positive and encouraging results. The second tool is constant dialogue with union representatives through periodic meetings. In the past year, each plant in Italy alone averaged more than one meeting every two months for a total of 21 meetings. At Group level, about 77% of employees are covered by union representation.

For the area relating to People, impacts related to the management of internal human resources and along the Value Chain were analyzed. The analysis considered aspects such as employee well-being and safety, working conditions, skill development, inclusion, and equal treatment. Among the potentially relevant impacts in the HR field, work-related stress was identified as being monitored through a biennial questionnaire and analysis of the results obtained.
As an action for 'Worker stress due to inadequate strategic work planning process and lack of interpersonal relationships in the company', the company states 'Business climate analysis Agile work'.

For 'Diversity', the action listed is 'Promote an inclusive culture and support diversity by educating staff to respect human rights and addressing gender, ethnic and cultural biases through the adoption of ethical standards, business strategies and goals (Gender Equality certification, Disal training).'
score_explanation_french: L'entreprise obtient 100 points car ses divulgations témoignent d'une approche complète de l'engagement des employés. Elle décrit plusieurs mécanismes d'engagement, notamment une enquête d'entreprise annuelle (analyse du climat) dans diverses zones géographiques (Slovénie et Croatie en 2024), des questionnaires biennaux sur le stress lié au travail et des réunions périodiques avec les représentants syndicaux (21 réunions en Italie, couvrant 77% des employés du Groupe). L'entreprise déclare explicitement que les résultats de ces enquêtes, telles que l'« analyse du climat des affaires », éclairent des actions directes comme le « travail agile » pour atténuer le stress des travailleurs. En outre, les domaines d'engagement comme la « Diversité » sont liés à des actions spécifiques comme la formation DE&I et la certification pour l'égalité des sexes, ce qui indique un lien clair entre le feedback/l'identification des problèmes et les actions ultérieures. Cela remplit tous les critères pour le score « Chef de file ».
score_explanation_english: The company scores 100 points because its disclosure demonstrates a comprehensive approach to employee engagement. It describes multiple engagement mechanisms, including an annual Corporate Survey (climate analysis) in various geographical areas (Slovenia and Croatia in 2024), biennial questionnaires for work-related stress, and periodic meetings with union representatives (21 meetings in Italy, covering 77% of employees across the Group). The company explicitly states that results from these surveys, such as the 'Business climate analysis,' inform direct actions like 'Agile work' to mitigate worker stress. Furthermore, engagement areas like 'Diversity' are linked to specific actions like DE&I training and Gender Equality certification, indicating a clear link between feedback/identification of issues and subsequent actions. This fulfills all criteria for the 'Leading' score.
score_explanation_italian: L'azienda ottiene 100 punti perché la sua informativa dimostra un approccio completo al coinvolgimento dei dipendenti. Descrive molteplici meccanismi di coinvolgimento, tra cui una "Corporate Survey" annuale (analisi del clima) in diverse aree geografiche (Slovenia e Croazia nel 2024), questionari biennali per lo stress lavoro-correlato e incontri periodici con i rappresentanti sindacali (21 incontri in Italia, che coprono il 77% dei dipendenti del Gruppo). L'azienda dichiara esplicitamente che i risultati di queste indagini, come la "Business climate analysis," ispirano azioni dirette come il "lavoro Agile" per mitigare lo stress dei lavoratori. Inoltre, aree di coinvolgimento come la "Diversity" sono collegate ad azioni specifiche come la formazione DE&I e la certificazione Gender Equality, indicando un chiaro collegamento tra il feedback/l'identificazione delle problematiche e le azioni successive. Ciò soddisfa tutti i criteri per il punteggio "Leading".

user_friendly_explanation_italian: L'azienda ha fornito un resoconto molto dettagliato del suo consumo energetico per l'anno 2024. Dichiara chiaramente la quantità totale di energia consumata, delineando con precisione quanta parte provenga da fonti pulite e rinnovabili, come l'energia eolica e solare, e quanta dai combustibili fossili. Spiega anche quali settori della sua attività sono inclusi in queste cifre, garantendo chiarezza e trasparenza. Questa rendicontazione completa ci aiuta a comprendere chiaramente la loro impronta ambientale.
user_friendly_explanation: The company has provided a very detailed account of its energy use for the year 2024. They clearly state the total amount of energy consumed, precisely outlining how much came from clean, renewable sources like wind and solar, and how much came from fossil fuels. They also explain which parts of their business are included in these figures, ensuring clarity and transparency. This comprehensive reporting helps us understand their environmental footprint clearly.
information: In 2024, the Group consumed a total of 682,803 MWh of energy, 68% of which came from fossil sources and 32% from certified renewable sources, including hydroelectric, wind and photovoltaic. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 49]

The 2024 reporting scope includes all companies in the financial consolidation of Aquafil. The companies Acca, Poly, Aquafil India, and Nofir, which are reported in the Sustainability Report as 'Holdings', are excluded from the energy mix scope (and emissions calculation) as Aquafil has no operational control over them. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 252]

The detailed breakdown of energy consumption for 2024 includes: Fuel consumption from crude oil and petroleum products (12,797 MWh, 1.9% of total), Fuel consumption from natural gas (429,087 MWh, 62.8% of total), Consumption of purchased or acquired electricity, heat, steam and cooling from fossil sources (22,453 MWh, 3.3% of total), Total energy consumption from fossil sources (464,336 MWh, 68% of total). [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 49]

For renewable sources, the breakdown includes: Fuel consumption for renewable sources, including biomass* (1 MWh, 0% of total), Consumption of purchased or acquired electricity, heat, steam, and cooling from renewable sources (213,422 MWh, 31% of total), Consumption of self-generated non-fuel renewable energy (5,043 MWh, 1% of total), Total renewable energy consumption (218,466 MWh, 32% of total). [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 49]

The report fulfils the requirements of the European Directive 2022/2464 on the Sustainability Statement (Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive - CSRD), implemented in Italy by Legislative Decree No. 2024/125, and has been prepared according to the European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS). There is a change in scope from the previous year, which was extended to align with the consolidated financial statements. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 82]

user_friendly_explanation_italian:
Aquafil condivide apertamente le proprie emissioni dirette di gas serra, note come emissioni Scope 1, per il 2024. Tali emissioni ammontano a 91.347 tonnellate metriche di anidride carbonica equivalente, provenienti da operazioni quali i suoi impianti di produzione e gli uffici. L'azienda utilizza linee guida internazionali (GHG Protocol) e i propri strumenti interni per misurare con precisione tali emissioni. Ciò significa che traccia esattamente la quantità di emissioni di cui è responsabile dalle proprie attività, garantendo che i dati siano affidabili e coerenti. Questo livello di trasparenza aiuta tutti a comprendere l'impatto ambientale diretto di Aquafil e mostra il loro impegno per una rendicontazione accurata.

*   **Emissioni Quantificate:** L'azienda fornisce un numero preciso per le proprie emissioni dirette.
*   **Unità Chiare:** Le emissioni sono riportate in unità standard (tonnellate metriche di CO2 equivalente).
*   **Ambito Definito:** Le emissioni coprono tutte le fonti direttamente possedute o controllate in tutti i loro siti di produzione globali.
*   **Metodi Trasparenti:** I metodi di calcolo sono dettagliati e seguono standard internazionali consolidati.
user_friendly_explanation:
Aquafil openly shares its direct greenhouse gas emissions, known as Scope 1 emissions, for 2024. These emissions total 91,347 metric tons of carbon dioxide equivalent, coming from operations like its production plants and offices. The company uses international guidelines (GHG Protocol) and its own internal tools to precisely measure these emissions. This means they track exactly how much pollution they're responsible for from their own activities, ensuring the data is reliable and consistent. This level of transparency helps everyone understand Aquafil's direct environmental impact and shows their commitment to accurate reporting. 

*   **Quantified Emissions:** The company provides a precise number for its direct emissions.
*   **Clear Units:** Emissions are reported in standard units (metric tons of CO2 equivalent).
*   **Defined Scope:** The emissions cover all directly owned or controlled sources across their global production sites.
*   **Transparent Methods:** The calculation methods are detailed and follow established international standards.
information: Emissions are calculated monthly by converting the amounts of energy consumed into carbon dioxide equivalent (CO2 eq). For their measurement, we refer to the GHG Protocol classification system, which divides emissions into Scope 1, Scope 2 and Scope 3. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 51]
Gross GHG Emissions - Scope 1, Unit = tCO₂eq, 2024 = 91,347. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 52]

Greenhouse gas emissions are calculated monthly by converting the amounts of energy consumed into carbon dioxide equivalent (CO2 eq). For this purpose, a specially developed calculation tool, the Sustainability Web Tool, is used to which most production plants have access and which uses energy carrier-specific emission factors. In 2024, there was no change in quantification methods other than the adoption of the Inventory Management Plan, which standardised different plants' approaches to conversions and data sharing, ensuring compliance with CRSD requirements (including the use of PCI in energy conversion). level_2_metrics.metric_name: Pollution Of Air Water And Soil

Hanno anche sistemi per identificare e gestire i rischi di inquinamento, garantendo la conformità alle leggi ambientali e l'uso di tecnologie speciali per depurare le acque reflue. level_3_metrics.metric_name: Pollution Emissions To Air Water And Soil
user_friendly_explanation: Aquafil is doing a great job in managing and reporting its pollution. They have clear plans and policies in place to reduce pollution, actively share detailed numbers about the pollutants they release into water, and are even leaders in developing new ways to measure plastic pollution. They also have systems to identify and manage pollution risks, ensuring they comply with environmental laws and use special technologies to clean wastewater. This means they are working hard to protect our air, water, and soil from harmful substances.
information: Definition of a Water Policy [Source: Investor Presentation, Page 12]
Environmental Policy approval [Source: Investor Presentation, Page 7]
Environmental Policy and ESG Policy – which codify, among other environmental goals, a commitment to reduce water consumption and pollution. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 77]

With the new Environmental Policy, we are committed to pollution prevention and control through actions such as the adoption of Environmental Management System (EMS) throughout the Group by 2025. We have also equipped some plants with Integrated Environmental Authorization and Single Territorial Authorization, establishing specific emission limits. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 77]

Water Policy: commitments to efficient water use, safeguarding water resources, reducing water waste, optimizing water use, and specifically aims at 'reduction of residual pollutants in water discharges'. [Source: Water Policy – Aquafil Group, Page 4]
Certify all Group plants are ISO 14001 certified (environmental management). [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 26]

Aquafil has an Integrated Management System to ensure quality, environmental protection, energy efficiency, health and safety, social responsibility and gender equality in all Group companies. The policy includes alignment with international standards ISO 9001, ISO 14001, ISO 45001, ISO 50001, SA 8000, and ISO 30415. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 119]

TABLE 2.8 EMISSION OF POLLUTANTS TO WATER BY PLANTS THAT EXCEED THE E-PRTR REPORTING THRESHOLDS (2024): Nickel and compounds (Ni): 22.9 kg/year; Phenols (C): 188.1 kg/year; Total Organic Carbon TOC (C or COD/3): 312,906.7 kg/year. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 53]

In Section 2.3.2, the Group also voluntarily reports the Chemical Oxygen Demand (COD) values of its water discharges. In 2024, 58% of wastewater was discharged to surface water, with an average COD value of 22 mg of O2 per litre – well below the maximum threshold of 160 mg/l set by Italian national regulations and the more restrictive limit 100 mg/l set by the Autonomous Province of Trento. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 58]
In 2023, we developed a new international standard – ISO 4484-2 – to uniquely measure microplastics released from the textile sector. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 54]
Development, validation and issuance of ISO 4484-2 standard on microplastics [Source: Investor Presentation, Page 7]

We monitor the release of pollutants into the air, water and soil, strictly complying with the environmental regulations in force in the countries where we operate. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 53]

Each plant monitors its pollutant emissions and compliance with legal limits, which vary depending on the applicable local regulations and the specific activities performed. The calculation methodology involves two steps: third-party and independent companies perform their periodic analyses to measure concentrations; the plants then calculate the final value by multiplying the concentrations by the flow rate and hours of operation. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 53]
In 2024, the company allocated more than Euro 900,000 in CapEx and Euro 20,000 in OpEx spending to the pollution issue. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 53]

The Group has identified its material pollution-related impacts, risks, and opportunities through materiality analysis (see Section 1.4), which considers all of the Group's geographical areas and business activities, and the entire value chain. Importantly, no actual direct negative impacts were identified, only indirect ones due to value chain activities, on which we are beginning to strengthen controls through the EcoVadis project, described in Section 1.5.3 'EcoVadis Risk Mapping'. For this reason, we have not implemented or planned corrective actions related to pollution. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 53]

To reduce the input of organic pollutants into wastewater, we have implemented reverse osmosis systems. This is a purification technology that enables us to achieve COD values consistently below the limits set by law. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 58]

The next step will be to reduce the release of microplastics through the selection of more sustainable materials and the adoption of eco-design practices for garment creation. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 54]
score_explanation_french: L'entreprise obtient des scores élevés dans les trois catégories. Concernant les objectifs et les politiques, Aquafil dispose de politiques environnementales et hydriques approuvées et accessibles au public, décrivant des engagements spécifiques en matière de prévention et de réduction de la pollution, y compris des objectifs quantifiables comme la certification ISO 14001 pour toutes les usines d'ici 2025 (11/13 atteintes). Pour la divulgation des données de pollution, l'entreprise fournit des données quantitatives sur la pollution de l'eau (nickel, phénols, COT, DCO) pour les usines dépassant les seuils de déclaration E-PRTR et volontairement pour les usines américaines. Elle souligne également son rôle dans le développement de la norme ISO 4484-2 pour la mesure des microplastiques, ce qui témoigne d'une collecte de données solide. Pour la gestion des impacts et des risques, Aquafil surveille les émissions de polluants dans l'air, l'eau et le sol, assurant le respect des limites légales grâce à des analyses tierces et des calculs internes. Le système de gestion environnementale (certifié ISO 14001) est explicitement utilisé pour identifier, contrôler et atténuer les impacts environnementaux. Des mesures d'atténuation spécifiques pour les eaux usées, telles que les systèmes d'osmose inverse, sont en place, et les efforts de R&D sont axés sur la réduction des microplastiques. L'entreprise mène une analyse de matérialité pour identifier les impacts et les risques liés à la pollution, en mettant l'accent sur les impacts indirects à travers sa chaîne de valeur.
score_explanation_english: The company scores highly across all three categories. For Targets & Policies, Aquafil has publicly available, approved Environmental and Water Policies outlining specific commitments to pollution prevention and reduction, including quantifiable goals like ISO 14001 certification for all plants by 2025 (11/13 achieved). For Disclosure of Pollution Data, the company provides quantitative data on water pollution (Nickel, Phenols, TOC, COD) for plants exceeding E-PRTR thresholds and voluntarily for US plants. It also highlights its role in developing ISO 4484-2 for measuring microplastics, indicating robust data collection. For Impact & Risk Management, Aquafil monitors pollutant emissions to air, water, and soil, ensuring compliance with legal limits through third-party analyses and internal calculations. The Environmental Management System (ISO 14001 certified) is explicitly used to identify, control, and mitigate environmental impacts. Specific mitigation measures for wastewater, such as reverse osmosis systems, are in place, and R&D efforts are focused on microplastic reduction. The company conducts materiality analysis to identify pollution-related impacts and risks, emphasizing indirect impacts through its value chain.
score_explanation_italian: L'azienda ottiene punteggi elevati in tutte e tre le categorie. Per Obiettivi e Politiche, Aquafil ha Politiche Ambientali e Idriche pubblicamente disponibili e approvate che delineano impegni specifici per la prevenzione e la riduzione dell'inquinamento, inclusi obiettivi quantificabili come la certificazione ISO 14001 per tutti gli stabilimenti entro il 2025 (11/13 raggiunti). Per la Divulgazione dei Dati sull'Inquinamento, l'azienda fornisce dati quantitativi sull'inquinamento idrico (Nickel, Fenoli, TOC, COD) per gli stabilimenti che superano le soglie E-PRTR e volontariamente per gli stabilimenti USA. Sottolinea inoltre il suo ruolo nello sviluppo di ISO 4484-2 per la misurazione delle microplastiche, a testimonianza di una solida raccolta dati. Per la Gestione dell'Impatto e del Rischio, Aquafil monitora le emissioni di inquinanti nell'aria, nell'acqua e nel suolo, garantendo la conformità ai limiti legali attraverso analisi di terze parti e calcoli interni. Il Sistema di Gestione Ambientale (certificato ISO 14001) viene esplicitamente utilizzato per identificare, controllare e mitigare gli impatti ambientali. Specifiche misure di mitigazione per le acque reflue, come i sistemi ad osmosi inversa, sono in atto, e gli sforzi di R&S sono concentrati sulla riduzione delle microplastiche. L'azienda effettua un'analisi di materialità per identificare impatti e rischi legati all'inquinamento, enfatizzando gli impatti indiretti attraverso la sua catena del valore.

user_friendly_explanation_italian: Aquafil sta svolgendo un ottimo lavoro nel comprendere e gestire il proprio impatto sulla natura. L'azienda condivide apertamente l'ubicazione esatta delle sue 19 fabbriche distribuite in 13 paesi, inclusa la quantità di terreno coperto da edifici e quanto habitat naturale potrebbe essere influenzato. Hanno verificato quasi tutti i loro siti (83%) per valutarne la vicinanza ad aree naturali importanti e prevedono di coprire tutti i siti entro il prossimo anno. Aquafil ha anche piani chiari e azioni specifiche, come la gestione dell'illuminazione e del rumore, per ridurre qualsiasi danno che le loro operazioni potrebbero causare alla fauna selvatica e agli habitat locali. Si impegnano per il miglioramento continuo e stabiliranno obiettivi ancora più dettagliati per la protezione della biodiversità in futuro.
level_3_metrics.metric_name: Biodiversity Impact Number And Area Of Business Sites Near Sensitive Areas
user_friendly_explanation: Aquafil is doing an excellent job in understanding and managing its impact on nature. The company openly shares exactly where its 19 factories are located across 13 countries, including how much land is covered by buildings and how much natural habitat might be affected. They have checked nearly all their sites (83%) for how close they are to important natural areas and plan to cover all of them by next year. Aquafil also has clear plans and specific actions, like managing lighting and noise, to reduce any harm their operations might cause to local wildlife and habitats. They are committed to continuous improvement and will be setting even more detailed goals for protecting biodiversity in the future.
information: Aquafil is a global Group, present in 13 countries around the world, and a leading manufacturer of synthetic fibres and polymers. The Group is present on three different continents, employing about 2,400 people at 19 production sites located in Italy, Slovenia, Unites States, China, Croatia, Chile, Thailand and Japan. [Source: RESULTS FOR THE FIRST NINE MONTHS OF 2025, Page 6]
Appendix 5.1 'Aquafil Facilities' provides a detailed list by country and specific location, including a brief description of their activity. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 127]
Total, Soil sealing (ha)* = 36.79. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 61]
Total, Habitat fragmentation (ha)** = 3.45. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 61]

The area of soil sealed off due to cementification coincides with the area occupied by each production plant in hectares. Habitat fragmentation occurs only where the plant is located in a natural area (or was in the past). Again, the impact is assessed by measuring the size of the plant in hectares. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 61]

The Group completed this assessment through a Biodiversity Impact and Risk Assessment, carried out on European and North American plants between 2023 and 2024, achieving 83% coverage of production plants. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 59]

Over the past year, the company has extended the scope of the analysis to its U.S. plants (Georgia, Arizona, North Carolina and California). Currently, the analysis covers 83% of the Group's production plants, and the idea is to reach full coverage in 2025*. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 47]

The Taxonomy DNSH criterion for Biodiversity calls for assessment for facilities 'close' to areas of high biodiversity content. Aquafil decided to consider areas within 10 km of its plants as 'close', a precautionary choice as usually a shorter distance (5 km) is selected for certifications such as EIA. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 61]

These areas, also called 'sensitive' areas, are identified through the Natura 2000 network for Europe or through the classification of the International Union for Conservation of Nature for the United States. The complete list of sensitive areas near Group plants can be found in Appendix 5.7. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 60]
Table 2.12 shows Aquafil's production sites within 10 km of sensitive areas. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 61]

Through its Environmental Policy (see section 2.1), the Aquafil Group is committed to protecting the environment in the different countries where it operates. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 59]

In the second stage, both direct and indirect impacts on biodiversity are assessed. The impact is considered direct when the construction of the plant contributes to the fragmentation or reduction of a natural area, or degradation of land. Indirect impact, on the other hand, is estimated based on several factors: - the number of sensitive areas present within a radius of 10 km and their distance from the plant; - artificial lighting, which can alter the balance of local flora and fauna, disorientating animals and affecting plant photosynthesis; - the presence of glass surfaces, a potential threat to birds in flight; - acoustic emissions, which can interfere with birds' sound communication, essential for reproduction and defence against predators; - other attractive elements, such as the creation of artificial ponds for aesthetic purposes. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 60]

We report some mitigation actions we have identified that can be applied across the board: - Artificial lighting: outdoors, use lamps with green or blue colours and beams of light directed downward at night. Illuminated signs or other non-security-related lights should be turned off after 11 p.m. - Glazed surfaces: to prevent bird collisions with clear glass, apply bird silhouette stickers at a density of 1 per m². - Noise emissions: all plants in the European scope meet the limits imposed by law. However, considering the continuity of sound emissions (almost all facilities produce sound 24/7), it is advisable to create sound-absorbing devices and sound barriers. - Other attractive factors: as no relevant factors have arisen, no mitigation measures are necessary. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 61]

On the other hand, in 2026, a strategic plan for reducing biodiversity impacts and mitigating transition risks will be prepared, which will also include future-oriented targets, concrete actions to achieve them and KPIs to monitor progress. Where necessary, an ad-hoc policy for biodiversity protection will also be possible. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 59]
Aquafil has implemented a set of mitigation solutions with a focus on those of an acoustic nature. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 77]
score_explanation_french: L'entreprise obtient 100 points pour le nombre et la surface des sites d'activité ayant un impact sur la biodiversité situés à proximité de zones sensibles. Dans la catégorie 1 (Divulgation des sites), Aquafil reçoit 30 points pour avoir communiqué le nombre total de ses 19 sites de production, leurs emplacements dans 13 pays sur trois continents, et des informations détaillées incluant la superficie totale imperméabilisée (36,79 ha) et la fragmentation totale de l'habitat (3,45 ha). Pour la catégorie 2 (Évaluation de l'exposition aux zones sensibles), Aquafil obtient 30 points car elle quantifie que son Évaluation d'impact et de risque sur la biodiversité (BIA) couvre 83 % de ses usines de production, avec un plan pour atteindre une couverture complète d'ici 2025. L'entreprise définit également les « zones proches des zones sensibles » comme étant dans un rayon de 10 km de ses usines et liste les sites spécifiques et les zones sensibles associées. Dans la catégorie 3 (Gestion des risques liés à la biodiversité), Aquafil obtient 40 points pour la mise en place de pratiques claires de gestion des risques liés à la biodiversité au niveau des sites. Elle détaille explicitement son engagement envers sa Politique environnementale, les méthodologies d'évaluation des impacts directs et indirects (par exemple, éclairage, surfaces vitrées, émissions acoustiques) et précise des actions d'atténuation concrètes. En outre, elle prévoit d'élaborer un plan stratégique pour les impacts sur la biodiversité avec des objectifs et des indicateurs de performance futurs d'ici 2026. Cette approche complète en matière de divulgation, d'évaluation et de gestion justifie l'attribution de tous les points dans toutes les catégories.

user_friendly_explanation: The company fully shares how much water it uses and where that water comes from. For 2024, they reported drawing a total of 1,876,848 cubic meters of water, and they explained that most of it (83%) came from underground sources like wells, some (16.5%) from public water systems (aqueducts), and a small amount (0.4%) from rivers.
information: In 2024, the Group withdrew and consumed a total of 1,876,848 m3 of water resources. This total is broken down by source as follows: 1,558,769 m3 (83%) from ground water (wells), 309,737 m3 (16.5%) from third-party water resources (aqueducts), and 8,342 m3 (0.4%) from surface water (rivers).

Nel 2024, il Gruppo ha prelevato e consumato quasi 1.900.000 m3 di risorse idriche di cui l'83% proveniente da acque sotterranee (pozzo), il 16,5% da risorse idriche di terzi (acquedotti) e solo lo 0,4% da acque superficiali (fiumi).

user_friendly_explanation_italian:
Aquafil è estremamente trasparente riguardo al suo utilizzo dell'acqua, mostrando chiaramente quanta acqua preleva e restituisce all'ambiente. Fornendo dettagliate analisi sull'origine e la destinazione dell'acqua, insieme a controlli di qualità regolari, l'azienda dimostra un forte impegno per la trasparenza. Utilizzano inoltre strumenti specializzati per monitorare la loro impronta idrica e fanno verificare i loro rapporti da esperti esterni, il che rappresenta una pratica all'avanguardia nella divulgazione ambientale.

Sul fronte della gestione, Aquafil ha un piano chiaro per la gestione dell'acqua, che include un team dedicato e persone in ogni stabilimento responsabili del monitoraggio dell'uso dell'acqua. Lavorano attivamente per ridurre il consumo di acqua attraverso tecnologie e programmi avanzati, e valutano i rischi legati all'acqua come la scarsità. Sebbene mirino a ridurre l'assunzione complessiva di acqua di una certa percentuale entro il 2030, tale percentuale specifica è ancora in fase di definizione. Nonostante ciò, il loro approccio completo alla gestione dell'acqua e alla valutazione dei rischi mostra un alto livello di dedizione alle pratiche sostenibili.

Spiegazione del termine "COD": COD sta per Chemical Oxygen Demand. È una misurazione utilizzata per valutare la quantità di inquinamento organico nell'acqua, indicando quanto ossigeno sarebbe necessario per scomporre gli inquinanti in una reazione chimica. Un valore di COD inferiore indica tipicamente acqua più pulita.
user_friendly_explanation:
Aquafil is highly open about its water use, clearly showing how much water it takes in and puts back into the environment. By providing detailed breakdowns of where the water comes from and where it goes, along with regular quality checks, the company demonstrates a strong commitment to transparency. They also use specialized tools to track their water footprint and have their reports checked by outside experts, which is a leading practice in environmental disclosure.

On the management side, Aquafil has a clear plan for managing water, including a dedicated team and people at each facility responsible for monitoring water use. They actively work on reducing water consumption through advanced technologies and programs, and they assess water-related risks like scarcity. While they aim to reduce overall water intake by a certain percentage by 2030, that specific percentage is still being worked out. Despite this, their comprehensive approach to water management and risk assessment shows a high level of dedication to sustainable practices.

Explanation of the term "COD": COD stands for Chemical Oxygen Demand. It's a measurement used to assess the amount of organic pollution in water, indicating how much oxygen would be needed to break down pollutants in a chemical reaction. A lower COD value typically means cleaner water.
information: Total Water Withdrawal: 1,876,848 m³ in 2024 [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 56, TABLE 2.9]
Total Water Discharge: 1,347,898 m³ in 2024 [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 58, TABLE 2.11]

Water Discharge Destinations (2024): 58% to surface water, 42% to third parties for purification [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 58]

Dedicated Monitors: A dedicated withdrawals monitor is designated at each plant to collect relevant data and report anomalies [Source: Report di Sostenibilità, Page 55]
Water Stress Assessment: Monitors water stress levels using WWF's Water Risk Filter; withdrawals and discharges mainly occur in medium-to-low-risk areas [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 57]

Water Reduction Targets: Objective to 'Reduce total water withdrawal by X% by 2030 (based on 2021)' but current progress is 'Ongoing: definition of base year and implementation of measures for its calculation' [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 25, TABLE 1.2]
Water Efficiency Initiatives: Optimization of polymerisation production processes to reduce energy and water consumption [Source: Consolidated Half-Year Financial Report, Page 27]
Wastewater Treatment: Reduction of organic pollutants in wastewater through reverse osmosis systems [Source: Water Policy - Aquafil Group, Page 5]
R&D Investments: Nearly Euro 40,000 CapEx in 2024 for improving water resource management [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 55]
Reporting Standards: The 2024 Sustainability Statement is prepared according to ESRS and is subject to limited assurance [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 9; Source: Annual Report, Page 174]
score_explanation_french:
L'entreprise divulgue publiquement son prélèvement total d'eau (1 876 848 m³) et ses rejets totaux d'eau (1 347 898 m³) pour 2024, ce qui permet le calcul de la consommation nette d'eau (528 950 m³). Cette divulgation comprend des ventilations quantitatives détaillées par source pour le prélèvement (eaux souterraines, ressources tierces, eaux de surface) et par destination pour le rejet (eaux de surface, tiers), ainsi que des paramètres de qualité de l'eau (valeurs de DCO). L'entreprise mentionne également des outils de suivi tels qu'une « carte de l'eau » et des indicateurs spécifiques pour la comparabilité dans le temps, et le rapport fait l'objet d'une assurance par un tiers. Ces éléments démontrent collectivement un niveau de transparence et de divulgation de pointe, ce qui lui vaut 100 points dans la catégorie 1.

En ce qui concerne la gestion, la gouvernance et la gestion des risques, l'entreprise a mis en œuvre une politique de l'eau spécifique en 2024 et a créé une équipe mondiale de l'eau (A.G.W.T.) dédiée, ainsi que des moniteurs de prélèvement au niveau de l'usine. Elle effectue des évaluations du stress hydrique à l'aide d'outils externes et met en œuvre diverses initiatives concrètes d'efficacité et de conservation de l'eau, telles que la collecte des eaux de pluie, les systèmes en boucle fermée et le traitement avancé des eaux usées. Cependant, bien qu'un objectif de « réduction du prélèvement total d'eau de X % d'ici 2030 (par rapport à 2021) » soit énoncé, le pourcentage spécifique (« X % ») n'est pas quantifié dans le tableau de rapport, et l'état d'avancement indique « En cours : définition de l'année de référence et mise en œuvre des mesures pour son calcul ». Cela signifie que l'objectif n'est pas encore entièrement mesurable, ce qui empêche un score parfait. Néanmoins, la structure de gouvernance étendue, les processus proactifs et les nombreuses initiatives justifient un score avancé de 75 points dans la catégorie 2.

Score final : (0.50 * 100) + (0.50 * 75) = 50 + 37.5 = 87.5 points.

These elements collectively demonstrate a leading level of transparency and disclosure, earning 100 points in Category 1.

For management, governance, and risk management, the company has implemented a specific Water Policy in 2024 and established a dedicated Aquafil Global Water Team (A.G.W.T.), along with plant-level withdrawal monitors. It conducts water stress assessments using external tools and implements various concrete water efficiency and conservation initiatives, such as rainwater harvesting, closed-loop systems, and advanced wastewater treatment. However, while a target to 'Reduce total water withdrawal by X% by 2030 (based on 2021)' is stated, the specific percentage ('X%') is not quantified in the reporting table, and the progress status indicates 'Ongoing: definition of base year and implementation of measures for its calculation'. This means the target is not yet fully measurable, preventing a perfect score. Nonetheless, the extensive governance structure, proactive processes, and numerous initiatives justify an advanced score of 75 points in Category 2.

Final Score: (0.50 * 100) + (0.50 * 75) = 50 + 37.5 = 87.5 points.

Questa divulgazione include ripartizioni quantitative dettagliate per fonte di prelievo (acque sotterranee, terzi, acque superficiali) e per destinazione di scarico (acque superficiali, terzi), insieme ai parametri di qualità dell'acqua (valori COD). L'azienda menziona anche strumenti di monitoraggio come una 'water map' e indicatori specifici per la comparabilità nel tempo, e il rapporto è sottoposto a verifica di terze parti. Questi elementi dimostrano collettivamente un livello di trasparenza e divulgazione all'avanguardia, ottenendo 100 punti nella Categoria 1.

Per la gestione, la governance e la gestione del rischio, l'azienda ha implementato una specifica Politica Idrica nel 2024 e ha istituito un Aquafil Global Water Team (A.G.W.T.) dedicato, insieme a monitor del prelievo a livello di stabilimento. Conduce valutazioni dello stress idrico utilizzando strumenti esterni e implementa diverse iniziative concrete di efficienza e conservazione idrica, come la raccolta dell'acqua piovana, sistemi a ciclo chiuso e un trattamento avanzato delle acque reflue. Tuttavia, sebbene sia dichiarato un obiettivo di 'Ridurre il prelievo idrico totale del X% entro il 2030 (sulla base del 2021)', la percentuale specifica ('X%') non è quantificata nella tabella di rendicontazione, e lo stato di avanzamento indica 'In corso: definizione dell'anno base e implementazione delle misure per il suo calcolo'. Ciò significa che l'obiettivo non è ancora completamente misurabile, impedendo un punteggio perfetto. Ciononostante, l'ampia struttura di governance, i processi proattivi e le numerose iniziative giustificano un punteggio avanzato di 75 punti nella Categoria 2.

user_friendly_explanation_italian: Aquafil è un leader nell'adozione di pratiche di economia circolare. Hanno piani e obiettivi chiari per utilizzare più materiali riciclati e riutilizzare i prodotti, e monitorano da vicino i loro progressi. Il loro prodotto principale, il nylon ECONYL®, è realizzato da rifiuti come vecchie reti da pesca e tappeti, e può essere riciclato più e più volte, dimostrando che la circolarità è al centro della loro attività a livello mondiale. Investono molto in nuove idee e collaborano con numerosi partner per sviluppare nuovi modi di riciclare e progettare prodotti affinché durino più a lungo e siano facilmente riutilizzabili. Tutte queste informazioni sono condivise apertamente con numeri e dettagli nei loro rapporti. Questi sforzi non sono solo piccoli progetti; fanno parte del modo in cui Aquafil opera in tutte le sue fabbriche e attività commerciali in tutto il mondo.
user_friendly_explanation: Aquafil is a leader in adopting circular economy practices. They have clear plans and goals to use more recycled materials and reuse products, and they track their progress closely. Their main product, ECONYL® nylon, is made from waste like old fishing nets and carpets, and it can be recycled over and over again, showing that circularity is at the heart of their business worldwide. They invest heavily in new ideas and work with many partners to develop new ways of recycling and designing products to last longer and be easily reused. All of this information is shared openly with numbers and details in their reports. These efforts are not just small projects; they are part of how Aquafil operates across all its factories and business activities around the globe.
information: Aquafil has a publicly disclosed circular economy strategy with clear, measurable goals, formalized in "The ECO PLEDGE®" with five guiding principles, including "Rethinking of products from a circular perspective." [Source: Corporate Governance and Ownership Structure Report, Page 10]

Targets include generating 60% of total turnover from ECONYL® branded products by 2025 (54.8% achieved by Dec 31, 2024), collecting 35,000 tonnes of post-consumer waste by 2025 (19,002t collected by Dec 31, 2024), and achieving 50% pallet reuse for EMEA BCF business by 2025 (44% achieved by Dec 31, 2024). [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 25], [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 26]

The core of Aquafil's operational integration is the ECONYL® Regeneration System, a closed-loop system for nylon. This system involves rescuing waste (fishing nets, fabric scraps, carpet flooring, industrial plastic globally), sorting and cleaning nylon, radical regeneration and purification, processing into yarns and polymers for various industries, and designing products for infinite recyclability. [Source: Investor Presentation, Page 6]

Aquafil is a global group with 19 production sites across 13 countries, and its circular economy commitment through the ECONYL® system is recognized and implemented across these operations. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 4], [Source: ISSUANCE OF NON-CONVERTIBLE BOND LOAN FOR €50 MILLION, Page 1]

Specific circular practices include the Born Regenerated to be Regenerable (R2R) program for co-designing recyclable carpets with customers and the development of the first fully circular fishing net from ECONYL®. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 6]

Aquafil has undertaken vertical integration upstream in the supply chain for post-consumer waste collection, including Aquafil Carpet Collection in the U.S. and investments in Nofir (Norway) and Aquafil Cile for fishing net recovery. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 16]

The company invests significantly in R&D (€8.2 million in 2024, 1.5% of sales) for pioneering circular solutions, such as MAGRITTE for ECONYL®-based 3D printing, a new PET chemical recycling process with a pilot plant in Arco, and a pilot plant for multilayer carpet separation in Rovereto. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 17], [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 18]

Aquafil actively collaborates with international partners, including universities, research institutes, customers, and industry bodies (UNI, CEN, ISO), to enable circularity and develop shared standards. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 19], [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 71], [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 103]

Examples of collaboration include developing the ISO 4484-2:2023 standard for measuring microplastics and the EN 17988:2024 series for circular fishing nets. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 19]

Quantitative data is regularly disclosed, including 54.8% of fibre sales from ECONYL® products in 2024, 19,002 tonnes of post-consumer waste collected in 2024, and 44% pallet reuse in 2024. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 25], [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 26]

Other metrics include 4.7% biological materials in total raw materials, 99.7% recyclable content in output products, and a breakdown of total waste generated (16,748,201 kg in 2024) by end-of-life destination, with 35% non-recycled waste. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 64], [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 173], [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 176], [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 177]

The company's sustainability reporting adheres to European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS) and includes Environmental Product Declarations (EPDs) based on ISO 14025. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 88], [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 121]

Circular practices are deeply embedded in Aquafil's global operations, spanning its 19 production sites across 13 countries. The ECONYL® Regeneration System is the cornerstone of its business model. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 4], [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 14]

The company's approach includes global waste collection networks, vertical integration for key waste streams (carpets, fishing nets), and group-wide certifications like ISO 14001, ISO 50001, and SA 8000, ensuring broad implementation of environmental and social standards. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 16], [Source: Annual Report, Page 70], [Source: Integrated Management System, Page 3]
score_explanation_french: Aquafil fait preuve d'un engagement fort envers les principes de l'économie circulaire dans toutes les catégories évaluées. L'entreprise dispose d'une stratégie d'économie circulaire divulguée publiquement avec des objectifs clairs et mesurables en matière de contenu recyclé, de collecte des déchets et de réutilisation des matériaux. La circularité est profondément intégrée dans son activité principale via le système de régénération ECONYL®, couvrant l'ensemble de la chaîne de valeur, de la collecte des déchets à la conception des produits et à leur recyclabilité infinie. Des investissements importants en R&D et une collaboration avec diverses parties prenantes stimulent l'innovation dans les matériaux et processus circulaires. L'entreprise fournit des données quantitatives complètes sur diverses métriques de circularité, en conformité avec les normes ESRS et en utilisant des EPD. Enfin, ces pratiques circulaires sont largement répandues dans l'ensemble de ses opérations mondiales, ce qui témoigne d'une échelle et d'une intégration significatives.
score_explanation_english: Aquafil demonstrates a strong commitment to circular economy principles across all evaluated categories. The company has a publicly disclosed circular economy strategy with clear, measurable goals for recycled content, waste collection, and material reuse. Circularity is deeply embedded in its core business through the ECONYL® Regeneration System, spanning the entire value chain from waste collection to product design and infinite recyclability. Extensive investment in R&D and collaboration with diverse stakeholders drive innovation in circular materials and processes. The company provides comprehensive quantitative data on various circularity metrics, aligning with ESRS standards and using EPDs. Finally, these circular practices are widespread across its global operations, indicating significant scale and integration.
score_explanation_italian: Aquafil dimostra un forte impegno ai principi dell'economia circolare in tutte le categorie valutate. L'azienda ha una strategia di economia circolare resa pubblica con obiettivi chiari e misurabili per il contenuto riciclato, la raccolta dei rifiuti e il riutilizzo dei materiali. La circolarità è profondamente integrata nel suo core business attraverso l'ECONYL® Regeneration System, che copre l'intera catena del valore dalla raccolta dei rifiuti alla progettazione del prodotto e alla riciclabilità infinita. Ampi investimenti in R&S e la collaborazione con diverse parti interessate promuovono l'innovazione nei materiali e nei processi circolari. L'azienda fornisce dati quantitativi completi su varie metriche di circolarità, in linea con gli standard ESRS e utilizzando gli EPD. Infine, queste pratiche circolari sono diffuse in tutte le sue operazioni globali, indicando una portata e un'integrazione significative.

user_friendly_explanation_italian: L'azienda è molto trasparente riguardo ai suoi rifiuti, fornendo informazioni dettagliate su quanto rifiuto genera, quali tipi di rifiuti sono (inclusi quelli pericolosi e non pericolosi) e cosa succede loro dopo l'uso. Monitorano il rifiuto totale, forniscono suddivisioni per diversi materiali come plastica e carta e mostrano quanto viene riciclato, inviato in discarica o incenerito. Condividono anche i loro sforzi e progetti per ridurre i rifiuti tramite design innovativi e partnership per raccogliere e riutilizzare materiali come reti da pesca e tappeti. Questo approccio completo significa che gestiscono e rendicontano attivamente i loro rifiuti, puntando a un'economia più circolare.
user_friendly_explanation: The company is very open about its waste, providing detailed information on how much waste it generates, what types of waste they are (including hazardous and non-hazardous), and what happens to them after use. They track total waste, provide breakdowns by different materials like plastic and paper, and show how much is recycled, sent to landfills, or incinerated. They also share their efforts and projects to reduce waste through innovative designs and partnerships to collect and reuse materials like fishing nets and carpets. This comprehensive approach means they are actively managing and reporting on their waste, aiming for a more circular economy.

Aquafil is a pioneer in the circular economy also thanks to the ECONYL® regeneration system, an innovative and sustainable process able to create new products from waste and give life to an endless cycle. The nylon waste is collected in locations all over the world and includes industrial waste but also products - such as fishing nets and rugs - that have reached the end of their useful life. Such waste is processed to obtain a raw material - caprolactam - with the same chemical and performance characteristics as those from fossil sources. The polymers produced from ECONYL® caprolactam are distributed to the Group's production plants, where they are transformed into yarn for rugs carpet flooring and for clothing. [Source: H1 2025 RESULTS, Page 7]

score_explanation_french: L'entreprise divulgue intégralement la production de déchets, ventilée par catégories dangereuses et non dangereuses, ainsi que par composition et destination en fin de vie. Par exemple, la quantité totale de déchets générés en 2024 était de 16 748 281 kg, dont 1 628 557 kg de déchets dangereux et 15 119 644 kg de déchets non dangereux. Des ventilations détaillées par composition (par exemple, plastique, papier, déchets de processus chimiques) et par méthode d'élimination (par exemple, recyclage, mise en décharge, incinération) sont également fournies. De plus, l'entreprise décrit son engagement à réduire les déchets par des initiatives d'économie circulaire, des projets d'éco-conception et des objectifs spécifiques de collecte de déchets post-consommation et de réutilisation des palettes. Ce niveau de détail, comprenant des données quantitatives complètes et des informations qualitatives étendues, correspond parfaitement aux critères du score le plus élevé.
score_explanation_english: The company provides full disclosure on waste generation, disaggregated into hazardous and non-hazardous categories, as well as by composition and end-of-life destination. For example, the total waste generated in 2024 was 16,748,281 kg, comprising 1,628,557 kg of hazardous waste and 15,119,644 kg of non-hazardous waste. Detailed breakdowns by composition (e.g., plastic, paper, chemical process waste) and by disposal method (e.g., recycling, landfill, incineration) are also provided. Furthermore, the company discusses its commitment to waste reduction through circular economy initiatives, eco-design projects, and specific targets for post-consumer waste collection and pallet reuse. This level of detail, including both comprehensive quantitative data and extensive qualitative information, fully aligns with the criteria for the highest score.

Vengono inoltre fornite suddivisioni dettagliate per composizione (ad es. plastica, carta, rifiuti di processi chimici) e per metodo di smaltimento (ad es. riciclo, discarica, incenerimento). Inoltre, l'azienda illustra il suo impegno per la riduzione dei rifiuti attraverso iniziative di economia circolare, progetti di eco-design e obiettivi specifici per la raccolta dei rifiuti post-consumo e il riutilizzo dei pallet. Questo livello di dettaglio, che include sia dati quantitativi esaustivi che ampie informazioni qualitative, si allinea pienamente con i criteri per il punteggio più alto.

user_friendly_explanation_italian: Aquafil ottiene il punteggio più alto perché fornisce un rapporto molto dettagliato delle sue emissioni di 'Scope 3', che sono le emissioni indirette di gas serra derivanti dall'intera sua catena di valore, includendo sia le attività a monte che a valle. Mostrano dati numerici reali per quasi tutte le categorie pertinenti, spiegando come hanno calcolato tali numeri e da dove hanno ottenuto i loro dati. Inoltre, un revisore indipendente ha verificato e confermato l'affidabilità di queste informazioni.
user_friendly_explanation: Aquafil gets the highest score because it provides a very detailed report of its 'Scope 3' emissions, which are the indirect greenhouse gas emissions from its entire business chain, including both upstream and downstream activities. They show actual numbers for almost all relevant categories, explaining how they calculated these numbers and from where they got their data. On top of that, an independent auditor has checked and confirmed the reliability of this information.
information: Since 2023, Aquafil has been monitoring Scope 3 emissions, a key step in reducing impact along the entire value chain. In 2024, Aquafil developed an Inventory Management Plan, bringing its calculation of emissions into line with the GHG Protocol, harmonising conversion methods and data sharing among its plants. The company also started reporting Scope 3 emissions in 2023.
Aquafil explicitly states that Scope 3 emissions are indirect GHG emissions related to its value chain, covering both upstream and downstream activities.
For 2024, Aquafil disclosed quantitative Scope 3 emissions totaling 932,467 tCO2eq. This includes detailed reporting for numerous categories:
- Purchased goods and services: 699,551 tCO2eq
- Capital goods: 12,894 tCO2eq
- Fuel and energy-related activities (not included in Scope 1 or Scope 2): 30,142 tCO2eq
- Upstream transportation and distribution: 41,330 tCO2eq
- Waste generated in operations: 3,163 tCO2eq
- Business travelling: 1,740 tCO2eq
- Employee commuting: 4,461 tCO2eq
- Downstream transportation: 25,958 tCO2eq
- End-of-life treatment of sold products: 111,721 tCO2eq
- Downstream leased assets: 609 tCO2eq
- Investments: 899 tCO2eq

Some categories like 'Upstream leased assets', 'Use of sold products', and 'Franchising' are reported as N/A, while 'Processing of sold products' is reported as 'not significant', with explanations provided for these exclusions.

The quantification approaches used for different Scope 3 categories include site-specific, spend-based, average product, distance-based, and waste-type-specific methods. Emission factors are sourced from recognized databases such as Ecoinvent, Bilan carbon (Ademe), IEA, and EXIOBASE/EPA.

Aquafil acknowledges that it 'does not use primary data obtained from suppliers in the calculation of Scope 3 emissions' but notes that 'supplier engagement began in 2024 but is currently being evaluated'.

The consolidated sustainability statement for the year ended December 31, 2024, which includes GHG emissions reporting, has undergone a 'limited assurance engagement' by PWC. This assurance confirms that the statement complies with the European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS), which mandates assurance for sustainability reporting.
score_explanation_french: L'entreprise obtient un score de 100 pour le rapport sur les émissions de Scope 3. Aquafil fournit un rapport complet, par catégorie, de ses émissions de Scope 3 pour 2024, détaillant les données quantitatives pour la plupart des catégories matérielles, conformément à la norme d'entreprise du protocole GHG. L'entreprise indique explicitement les méthodologies utilisées, y compris les approches de quantification spécifiques et les bases de données des facteurs d'émission, démontrant ainsi la robustesse méthodologique. De manière cruciale, la déclaration de durabilité consolidée, qui contient ces données de Scope 3, a fait l'objet d'un « engagement d'assurance limitée » de la part d'un cabinet d'audit indépendant (PWC), remplissant ainsi l'exigence de vérification pour un score maximal.

The company explicitly states the methodologies used, including specific quantification approaches and emission factor databases, demonstrating methodological robustness. Crucially, the consolidated sustainability statement, which contains this Scope 3 data, has received 'limited assurance engagement' from an independent audit firm (PWC), fulfilling the verification requirement for a top score.

L'azienda dichiara esplicitamente le metodologie utilizzate, includendo approcci specifici di quantificazione e database di fattori di emissione, dimostrando robustezza metodologica. user_friendly_explanation_italian: Aquafil prende molto sul serio la preparazione ai cambiamenti climatici. Hanno un piano chiaro per comprendere e affrontare i rischi climatici, come il caldo estremo o la siccità, nella maggior parte dei loro stabilimenti e presto li coprirà tutti. Utilizzano previsioni dettagliate, inclusi diversi scenari di riscaldamento elaborati da scienziati del clima, per capire cosa potrebbe accadere e come rispondere. L'azienda sta anche elaborando un piano a lungo termine per ridurre il proprio impatto climatico, in linea con gli obiettivi globali di limitare il riscaldamento a 1.5°C, e questo piano è collegato al modo in cui gestiscono la loro attività e prendono decisioni finanziarie. Sono trasparenti riguardo ai loro sforzi, seguendo standard di reporting di alto livello.
level_3_metrics.metric_name: Climate Adaptation Strategies And Risk Assessments
user_friendly_explanation: Aquafil is very serious about preparing for climate change. They have a clear plan to understand and deal with climate risks, like extreme heat or droughts, across most of their factories and will soon cover all of them. They use detailed forecasts, including different warming scenarios from climate scientists, to figure out what might happen and how to respond. The company is also creating a long-term plan to reduce their climate impact, in line with global goals to limit warming to 1.5°C, and this plan is connected to how they run their business and make financial decisions. They are transparent about their efforts, following high-level reporting standards.
information: Aquafil recognises the urgency of a transition to a low-emission economy . Over the past year we have implemented energy efficiency measures at our main production plant; developed an Inventory Management Plan , which provides uniform calculation of emissions under the GHG Protocol across the Group; and assessed the resilience of our business model to climate change risks through a Climate Risk and Vulnerability Assessment on our European and North American plant. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 5, Chunk 85]
In 2024, the company allocated more than Euro 9.7 million in CapEx to climate change mitigation. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 45, Chunk 128]

Through our Climate Risk and Vulnerability Assessment (CRVA), we identify the most significant physical risks to our operations and assess the resilience of our strategy and business model. This analysis, complementary to the materiality analysis, provides a more detailed picture of climate threats, enabling us to develop mitigation and adaptation strategies to protect our operations and value chain. The CRVA is a key first building block of the Group's resilience analysis, which will be integrated in the coming years. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 45, Chunk 130]

We are, in fact, developing a climate transition plan , in line with the Paris Agreement , with the goal of limiting global warming to 1.5°C within this century. The plan, to be published in 2027 (with 2026 as the base year), will include emission reduction targets and concrete actions to achieve them, and will be fully integrated with the Group's business strategy and financial planning. The plan will also identify and take into account major transition risks. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 46, Chunk 131]

In 2023, Aquafil conducted a Climate Risk and Vulnerability Assessment (CRVA) to assess its exposure and vulnerbility to (physical) climate risks in its European plants (Italy, Slovenia and Croatia) - as required by Taxonomy Regulation (EU) 2020/852. Over the past year, the company has extended the scope of the analysis to its U.S. plants (Georgia, Arizona, North Carolina and California). Currently, the analysis covers 83% of the Group's production plants, and the idea is to reach full coverage in 2025*. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 47, Chunk 132]

Based on this year's results, our timeline (see Figure 2.1) calls for the development of an adaptation plan for European plants by next year, which will also take into account key transition risks. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 47, Chunk 132]

The methodology adopted in the CRVA is that of scenario analysis. The risk is then assessed on two possible scenarios of differing severity, identified by the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC): the first medium-emission scenario foresees an increase in global average temperature from 2.1 °C to 3.5 °C; the second high-emission scenario foresees an increase from 3.3 °C to 5.7 °C - both over a medium- to long-term time horizon (40 to 80 years)**. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 47, Chunk 133]

In the first scenario, characterised by a more moderate increase in temperatures in the medium term, the risk for Aquafil remains predominantly medium to low , with a few exceptions involving mainly Slovenian plants, partly owing to the mitigation and adaptation measures already implemented. In the second scenario , where global warming is more pronounced, the level of risk increases for all the phenomena analysed. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 48, Chunk 135]

Drought, aridity and decreasing annual precipitation are further factors that should not be underestimated in Europe, as Aquafil's plants draw water from wells for industrial purposes, the availability of which could be reduced due to these phenomena. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 48, Chunk 135]

In line with the requirements of ESRS, a double materiality analysis was conducted for the first time, leading the Group to assess: - Impact materiality: the consequences - actual or potential - of the company's activities on people and the planet. - Financial materiality: the risks and opportunities in ESG that can affect financial and economic performance. All topics identified by the ESRS, and 31 subtopic were considered to be material, which were then reported on and analysed in chapters 2, 3 and 4. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 4, Chunk 84]

Currently, our emission reduction targets , in line with the Science-Based Targets initiative, are still being set. They will be an integral part of our climate transition plan , aligned with the goals of the Paris Agreement, due to be published in 2027 . [Source: Annual Report, Page 73, Chunk 520]

The improved rating was supported by several key initiatives, including: extending the Climate Risk & Vulnerability Assessment and the Biodiversity Impact Assessment across the entire Group; launching the ESG risk assessment program within the supply chain; enhancing sustainability reporting through the transition from GRI to ESRS standards ; achieving the UNI/PdR 125 certification for gender equality. [Source: https://www.aquafil.com/esg-agenda-2030, Chunk 1324]

ESG Policy, Defines Aquafil's sustainability principles and explains how they are integrated into its business model: Defines Aquafil's sustainability principles; Prescribes internal actions and commitments to achieve goalsi; Assigns roles and responsibilities in ESG governance. Impacts, risks and opportunities: E: climate change, pollution, water and marine resources, biodiversity and ecosystems, circular economy S: own workforce, workers in the value chain, affected communities, consumers and end-users G : business conduct. [Source: Annual Report, Page 62, Chunk 496]

ESG & Agenda 2030: Aquafil has always had a holistic approach towards sustainability, which extends beyond circular product design to encompass social and governance aspects too. [Source: www.aquafil.com, Chunk 1323]

The report fulfils the requirements of the European Directive 2022/2464 on the Sustainability Statement ( Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive - CSRD ), implemented in Italy by Legislative Decree No. 2024/125, and has been prepared according to the European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS). [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 4, Chunk 82]

The KPI numerator corresponds to the revenues defined as aligned according to the criteria provided by the EU Taxonomy. Specifically, the Group's turnover aligned with the Taxonomy are the total sum of the gross revenues from sales related to the activity 'ECONYL ® Regeneration System and other environmentally sustainable activities' (activity 3.17), those related to activity 2.3 referring to revenues from the sale of post-consumer material by Aquafil Carpet Collection and those related to activity 4.1 referring to revenues from the sale of energy produced by the photovoltaic system installed at the Aquafil CRO plant. The denominator of the indicator, on the other hand, corresponds to the total net revenues of the Group, as defined within the Consolidated Income Statement. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 187, Chunk 354]

CapEx: The numerator of the KPI, i.e. the aligned proportion of capital expenditures, corresponds to the amount of CapEx related to the activity 'ECONYL ® Regeneration System and other environmentally sustainable activities' and activity 2.3. This proportion was determined in two main ways: - in the case of expenses directly attributable to the activities described above, these were valued on the basis of the primary figure recorded; - in the case of expenses not directly attributable to the activity, the allocation of costs was made in proportion to the percentage of aligned turnover to total turnover. The total CapEx figures (KPI denominator) correspond to the increases for the year, as reported in the consolidated financial statements at December 31, 2024. In line with the provisions of point 1.1.2.1. of Annex I to the Delegated Regulation (EU) 2021/2178, CapEx also included expenses incurred on leased assets, accounted for in accordance with IFRS 16, and expenses incurred for sample development, accounted for in accordance with IAS 38. Both of these types of CapEx were allocated using the methodology outlined above. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 187, Chunk 355]

OpEx R&D - Research: The numerator of the KPI, i.e. research costs aligned with the Taxonomy refer to all project-related costs: - related to the development/optimisation of products relat -ed to the activity 'ECONYL ® Regeneration System and other environmentally sustainable activities'. - related to the development of new environmentally sustainable products/processes (e.g. use of natural pigments). Data on total research costs (denominator of the KPI) are derived from the reporting, internally, of all costs (i.e. personnel costs, consumables) related to the individual research projects described above. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 187, Chunk 356]

The report fulfils the requirements of the European Directive 2022/2464 on the Sustainability Statement ( Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive - CSRD ), implemented in Italy by Legislative Decree No. 2024/125, and has been prepared according to the European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS). There is a change in scope from the previous year, which was extended to align with the consolidated financial statements. Due to this change, comparative data for previous years are not available. The purpose of the materiality analysis is to identify the main positive and negative, actual and potential impacts, risks and opportunities generated or suffered by the Group directly (through its operations) or indirectly (through its value chain). In 2024, Aquafil revised and updated the analysis methodology, bringing it into line with the requirements of the new European CSRD directive and EFRAG guidelines. The main development since 2023 has been the introduction of the 'double materiality' principle, which has led the Group to assess: - Impact materiality : the consequences - actual or potential - of the company's activities on people and the planet. - Financial materiality : the risks and opportunities in the ESG arena that may affect Aquafil's financial performance, cash flow, and access to credit, and in terms of reputation, market posi -tioning, and competitive advantage. The materiality analysis is updated every two years, or following significant changes inside or outside the company. [Source: Sustainability Statement, Page 32, Chunk 118]

Sustainability is a key element of Aquafil's business strategy, and is deeply integrated with its internal strategy and processes. Aquafil is a global Group, present in 13 countries around the world, and a leading manufacturer of synthetic fibres and polymers. It is recognized for its commitment to the circular economy through the development of the ECONYL® Regeneration System, launched in 2011. This innovative process enables waste such as old carpets, textile scraps and fishing nets to be transformed into reclaimed nylon. Aquafil has always taken a holistic approach to sustainability , going beyond environmental protection to include social and governance aspects. This commitment has been formalized in the ECO PLEDGE®, a set of five principles that inspire all Group activities: 1. Rethinking of products from a circular perspective 2. Protect the environment 3. Sharing responsibilities along the supply chain 4. Protecting the well-being of individuals 5. Support local communities In 2023, an ESG Policy was also adopted, which defines Aquafil's sustainability principles and outlines their integration into our business model, ensuring a responsible approach targeting long-term sustainable growth. score_explanation_french: Aquafil démontre une approche globale et actionnable en matière de stratégies d'adaptation au climat et d'évaluation des risques. L'entreprise reconnaît explicitement les risques liés au changement climatique et l'urgence de la transition vers une économie à faibles émissions. Elle dispose d'un cadre bien structuré pour l'évaluation et l'intégration des risques climatiques, incluant les risques physiques et de transition. Aquafil mène une Évaluation des Risques et de la Vulnérabilité Climatiques (ERVC) sur l'ensemble de ses opérations, couvrant 83 % de ses usines de production en 2024, avec un objectif de couverture totale d'ici 2025. Cette évaluation utilise l'analyse de scénarios basée sur les données du GIEC, employant des scénarios à émissions moyennes (augmentation de 2,1 °C à 3,5 °C) et à fortes émissions (augmentation de 3,3 °C à 5,7 °C) sur un horizon à moyen et long terme (40-80 ans). Les résultats éclairent les stratégies d'atténuation et d'adaptation, et les risques identifiés tels que la hausse des températures, les vagues de chaleur, la sécheresse et les fortes précipitations sont traités. De plus, Aquafil élabore un plan de transition climatique, aligné sur l'objectif de 1,5 °C de l'Accord de Paris, dont la publication est prévue pour 2027. Ce plan comprendra des objectifs de réduction des émissions et des actions concrètes, entièrement intégrés dans la stratégie commerciale et la planification financière du Groupe. Les considérations liées au climat sont intégrées dans leur analyse de double matérialité, leur stratégie ESG et leurs structures de gouvernance, y compris des objectifs spécifiques liés à la rémunération des dirigeants. Leurs divulgations publiques, conformes aux ESRS, sont considérées comme équivalentes aux recommandations de la TCFD, ce qui indique un cadre de reporting robuste et transparent.
score_explanation_english: Aquafil demonstrates a comprehensive and actionable approach to climate adaptation and risk assessments. The company explicitly acknowledges climate change risks and the urgency of transitioning to a low-emission economy. They have a well-structured framework for assessing and integrating climate risks, including both physical and transitional risks. Aquafil conducts a Climate Risk and Vulnerability Assessment (CRVA) across its operations, covering 83% of its production plants in 2024 with a plan for full coverage by 2025. This assessment employs scenario analysis based on IPCC data, using both medium-emission (2.1°C to 3.5°C) and high-emission (3.3°C to 5.7°C) scenarios over a medium-to-long term horizon (40-80 years). The results inform mitigation and adaptation strategies, with identified risks such as rising temperatures, heatwaves, drought, and heavy rainfall being addressed. Furthermore, Aquafil is developing a climate transition plan, aligned with the Paris Agreement's 1.5°C goal, set for publication in 2027. This plan will include emission reduction targets and concrete actions, fully integrated into the Group's business strategy and financial planning. Climate-related considerations are embedded in their double materiality analysis, ESG strategy, and governance structures, including specific targets linked to executive remuneration. Their public disclosure, compliant with ESRS, is considered equivalent to TCFD recommendations, indicating a robust and transparent reporting framework.
score_explanation_italian: Aquafil dimostra un approccio completo e attuabile all'adattamento climatico e alla valutazione dei rischi. Aquafil effettua una Valutazione dei Rischi e della Vulnerabilità Climatica (CRVA) in tutte le sue operazioni, coprendo l'83% dei suoi stabilimenti di produzione nel 2024 con un piano per la copertura completa entro il 2025. Questa valutazione utilizza l'analisi di scenario basata sui dati IPCC, impiegando scenari sia a medie emissioni (da 2,1°C a 3,5°C) che ad alte emissioni (da 3,3°C a 5,7°C) su un orizzonte a medio-lungo termine (40-80 anni). I risultati guidano le strategie di mitigazione e adattamento, affrontando i rischi identificati quali l'aumento delle temperature, le ondate di calore, la siccità e le forti precipitazioni. Inoltre, Aquafil sta sviluppando un piano di transizione climatica, allineato all'obiettivo di 1,5°C dell'Accordo di Parigi, la cui pubblicazione è prevista per il 2027. Le considerazioni relative al clima sono incorporate nella loro analisi di doppia materialità, nella strategia ESG e nelle strutture di governance, inclusi obiettivi specifici collegati alla remunerazione dei dirigenti. La loro informativa pubblica, conforme agli ESRS, è considerata equivalente alle raccomandazioni TCFD, indicando un quadro di rendicontazione robusto e trasparente.
